data.title: Vision RNG secures $207M in financing from HASI
data.description:
• Vision RNG has closed on an aggregate $207M in project financing with HASI (NYSE:HASI), an investor in climate solutions.
  • This landmark funding will be instrumental in accelerating Vision RNG’s growth strategy, which will result in advancing sustainable energy solutions and reducing carbon emissions.
  • The financing package includes both a $130M construction facility and a $77M investment tax credit bridge loan, underscoring HASI's structuring capabilities as a preeminent investor in energy transition assets.
  • The funds will be used to develop facilities to convert landfill gas into renewable natural gas at the Ohio landfills of sustainable waste and recycling service provider WIN Waste Innovations.
  • The anticipated annual reduction of ~0.12M tons of fossil-based CO2 emissions underscores the environmental impact of this financing and the projects it will support.

data.title: Sienna Senior Living acquires continuing care homes portfolio in Alberta
data.description:
• Sienna Senior Living (TSX:SIA:CA) on Wednesday said it has acquired a portfolio of four continuing care homes in Alberta for an aggregate purchase price of $181.6M.
  • The acquisition is financed through $150M of CMHC debt with a weighted average interest rate of about 4.6% and a remaining average term of around 4.7 years.
  • The company expects investment yield of 6.5% during the first year of operations, with opportunity for additional growth.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: ASML leads semi maker stocks lower for second consecutive session
data.description:
ASML Holding (NASDAQ:ASML) shares slumped 6.4% by the time markets closed on Wednesday, one day after it accidentally released its financial report and outlook a day early.

Other semiconductor equipment makers followed suit for the second day in a row as well. Applied Material (NASDAQ:AMAT) and KLA Corporation (NASDAQ:KLAC) both slid about 3.5% while Lam Research (NASDAQ:LRCX) fell 3%. Nova Ltd. (NVMI) dropped 5%.

ASML, which produces the planet's most advanced semiconductor equipment, reduced its 2025 full-year sales outlook by 15%. Analysts connected the reduction in part to stricter export controls in the Netherlands for advanced semiconductor manufacturing equipment.

After China accounted for 49% of ASML's sales during the second quarter of 2024, those sales are expected to account for 20% in 2025.

ASML also pointed to continued low chip demand in the automotive and industrial sectors.

"With regards to market condition, while we continue to view AI as a key driver of the industry recovery with potential upside, we see other segments recovering more slowly than anticipated," said ASML CEO Christophe Fouquet during the company's earnings call. "The recovery will extend well into 2025, which is leading to customer cautiousness and some push outs in their investment."

"We all see that there is speculation around export controls," added ASML Chief Financial Officer Roger Dassen. "And also that is a driver for us to take a more cautious view on the China sales."

However, most semiconductor stocks recovered on Wednesday, including Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) which climbed 3%. Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) jumped nearly 5% while Marvell Technology (NASDAQ:MRVL) rose 2.8%. Arm Holdings gained 1.2%, while Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM), Analog Devices (ADI), Texas Instruments (TXN), Microchip Technology (MCHP) and Broadcom (AVGO) all increased by less than 1%.

AMD (AMD) slid 0.3%, while Intel (INTC) and Qualcomm (QCOM) both fell 1.5%.

data.title: Community Healthcare upsizes and extends maturity of $400M senior unsecured line of credit
data.description:
• Community Healthcare Trust (NYSE:CHCT) announced that it has closed on an amended 5-year $400 million senior unsecured revolving line of credit with the Second Amendment to the company's Third Amended and Restated Credit Agreement.
  • This amended $400 million Revolving Facility will significantly increase the Company's liquidity compared to the previous $150M senior unsecured revolving line of credit.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Rexford Industrial Realty slightly raises core FFO guidance after mixed Q3 results
data.description:
Rexford Industrial Realty (NYSE:REXR) slightly lifted its 2024 guidance for core funds from operations, which still fell short of the Wall Street consensus, on the heels of mixed results for Q3.

The Southern California-focused industrial REIT now sees company share of core FFO at $2.33-$2.35, up from the prior view of $2.32-$2.34. That still trails the $2.36 average analyst estimate.

Q3 company share of core FFO of $0.59, matching the consensus, ticked down from $0.60 in Q2 and rose from $0.56 in the year-earlier quarter.

Q3 total revenue of $241.8M, missing the $242.8M expected, advanced from $237.6M in the prior quarter and $205.4M a year before.

(REXR) shares slipped 0.8% in after-hours trading.

Same-property portfolio net operating income rose 2.6% from a year ago, and same-property portfolio cash NOI increased 5.3% Y/Y.

Average same-property portfolio occupancy stood at 96.9%, unchanged from the previous three-month period.

During the quarter, Rexford (REXR) completed two acquisitions for a total of $60.5M and sold one property for a total of $7.3M.

data.title: ,Crown Castle Q3 earnings beat, 2024 guidance affirmed, 7K nodes to be canceled
data.description:
Crown Castle (NYSE:CCI) reaffirmed its guidance for full-year earnings and revenue after Q3 results outpaced the Wall Street consensus. Its CEO also reported that it identified ~7,000 greenfield small cell nodes in its contracted backlog to a cancel in agreement with customers as part of its capital efficiency program.

The telecom infrastructure REIT reaffirmed its 2024 adjusted FFO per share guidance of $6.91-$7.02; its site rental revenue guidance at $6.32B-$6.36B vs. Visible Alpha estimate of $6.35B; and its adjusted EBITDA guidance of $4.14B-$4.19B previously vs. Visible Alpha consensus of $4.17B.

Q3 AFFO, revenue beat

Q3 AFFO per share of $1.84, topping the $1.70 consensus, rose from $1.62 in Q2 and $1.77 in last year's Q3.

"The third quarter was highlighted by 5.2% consolidated organic growth, excluding the impact of Sprint cancellations, as demand remained strong for our tower, small cell, and fiber solutions offerings," said Chief Financial Officer Dan Schlanger,

Net revenue of $1.65B, exceeding the average analyst estimate of $1.64B, climbed from $1.63B in the previous quarter slipped from $1.67B a year ago. Site rental revenue of $1.59B (matching the $1.59B Visible Alpha estimate) increased from $1.58B in Q2 and $1.58B in Q3 2023.

Adjusted EBITDA of $1.08B, beating the $1.05B Visible Alpha consensus, climbed from $1.01B in the prior quarter and $1.05B a year ago.

Node cancellations

CEO Steven Moskowitz said Crown Castle (CCI) completed talks with its customers in Q4 and jointly identified ~7,000 greenfield small cell nodes in its contracted backlog that they mutually agreed to cancel.

"These agreed upon cancellations increase the overall return of our remaining contracted backlog of approximately 40,000 nodes and improve our capital efficiency going forward," he said.

Crown Castle (CCI) stock fell 1.2% in after-hours trading.

Conference call at 5:00 PM ET.

Earlier, Crown Castle FFO of $1.84 beats by $0.14
